feedingProfile.preferredNpk: high-nitrogen balanced houseplant fertilizer (e.g. 24-8-16 or 10-10-10), spring through summer only

commonIssues: Root rot and leaf yellowing from overwatering or poor drainage — let soil dry slightly between waterings and never leave the pot standing in water
Sudden leaf drop from cold drafts, low light, overwatering, or temperatures below about 10-13 C
Pests: scale, mealybugs, aphids, thrips, and spider mites
Milky latex sap can irritate skin and is mildly toxic if ingested — keep away from pets and children, wash hands after pruning

In the wild this is a 15-30 m tree (hardy only in USDA zones 10-12); grown as a 1-3 m container houseplant elsewhere — the 10 L container minimum is for a young indoor plant, size up every 2-3 years as it grows
Reduce watering from fall to late winter; resume regular watering and feeding in spring

source.citation: Clemson University Home & Garden Information Center, 'Rubber Plant' factsheet — water thoroughly, let soil dry slightly between waterings; ensure drainage; root rot/yellowing from excess moisture
Clemson HGIC 'Rubber Plant' — ideal temperatures 75-80 F (24-27 C) day, 60-65 F (16-18 C) night; avoid below 55 F (13 C), sudden drops, and cold drafts

Clemson HGIC 'Rubber Plant' — prefers bright light, adaptable to low light, grows best with morning light from an east window; fertilize every two weeks with water-soluble houseplant fertilizer during spring/summer active growth

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder, Ficus elastica — USDA zones 10-12; 50-100 ft in habitat, 2-10 ft as houseplant; part shade / bright indirect light; medium water, water regularly in growing season, reduce fall to late winter; soil-based potting mix; pests: scale, aphids, mealybugs, thrips, spider mites

RHS, Ficus elastica (rubber plant) plant profile — hardiness H1b (grow outside only in summer, 10-15 C minimum); bright but filtered/indirect light; water moderately in growth, sparingly in winter; high-nitrogen fertiliser monthly; repot every 2-3 years

RHS Growing Guide, 'How to grow ornamental figs' — keep above 10-13 C (50-55 F); bright indirect light, tolerates a few hours of morning/evening sun; let compost dry slightly between waterings; loam-based compost (John Innes No 3) with fine bark; move to a slightly larger container every couple of years

Confidence note: sources give qualitative watering guidance ('let soil dry slightly between waterings', 'water moderately') with no day interval; 7 days is a conservative indoor estimate derived from that guidance. Light is specified as 'bright indirect' rather than sun-hours; 6 h target / 3-8 h range is an estimate mapping 'bright light, adaptable to low light, some morning sun'. No authoritative source states a pot volume; 10 L minimum (≈25 cm pot) is an estimate consistent with RHS 'slightly larger container every couple of years' repotting practice. heatStressC 30 is an estimate above the Clemson 24-27 C ideal day range.
